scenario.context: Station 02 of the day run: requirements specification for a user interface.
scenario.mission: You capture processes, structure requirements, and secure a clear picture of acceptance.

customerRequest.subject: Station 02: Specification
customerRequest.body: Please create a robust UI specification for ticket intake.

questions.title: Start the as-is analysis
questions.prompt: What belongs first in the as-is analysis?

options.text: Capture user tasks and process steps

feedback.correct: Correct: Process and user analysis come first.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Without an as-is picture, the basis for the specification is missing.

questions.title: Specification quality
questions.prompt: Which points improve the specification? (Multiple choice)

options.rationale: Performance and UX aspects become visible.

feedback.correct: Correct: The specification is built on a solid basis.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure measurability and scope clarity more strongly.

questions.title: From need to specification
questions.prompt: Put the flow to the finished specification in order.

feedback.correct: Correct: The flow is methodically sound.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Please go through analysis first, then formulation and release.

questions.title: Requirement volume
questions.prompt: There are 11 must-have requirements and 7 should-have requirements. How many requirements is that in total?

feedback.wrong: Please add the must-have and should-have requirements correctly.

questions.title: Specification note
questions.prompt: Formulate a specification note in 4-6 sentences with the goal, a core requirement, and an acceptance criterion.
questions.expectedOutline: Clear goal, prioritized requirement, and measurable criterion.

feedback.correct: Correct: The note is professionally robust.
feedback.wrong: Please formulate the goal, requirement, and criterion more concretely.
